description: Regulatory recognition data for plants in the archive. Three-tier system: WHO Monograph (evidence-based pharmacological documentation), EMA Traditional Use (European regulatory approval for traditional herbal medicines), FDA Approved (US drug approval or GRAS status). Sources: WHO Monographs Vols 1-4, EMA HMPC monographs, FDA Orange Book and GRAS database.

tiers.WHO: WHO Monograph on Selected Medicinal Plants — evidence-based pharmacological documentation with quality, safety, and efficacy assessment
tiers.EMA: European Medicines Agency HMPC approval — traditional use registration or well-established use authorisation for herbal medicines
tiers.FDA: US Food and Drug Administration — approved drug, GRAS (Generally Recognized As Safe), or qualified health claim
tiers.Cochrane: Cochrane Collaboration systematic review — independent evidence synthesis of clinical trials

fda.notes: Qualified health claim for cardiovascular risk

reviews: Garlic for hypertension (2012) — modest but significant reduction
Garlic for common cold prevention (2014) — significantly fewer colds

fda.notes: Not approved as drug; extensively studied

reviews: Ginkgo for cognitive decline (2009) — inconsistent results
Ginkgo for tinnitus (2013) — no significant benefit beyond placebo

plants.notes: Not currently in archive plant database — add as new entry

indications: prophylaxis and treatment of upper respiratory tract infections

fda.notes: Not approved as drug

reviews: Echinacea for preventing and treating the common cold (2015) — some preparations reduce incidence; effect size modest

indications: relief of mild symptoms of mental stress

fda.notes: Not approved as drug

reviews: Valerian for sleep quality (2006) — inconsistent results; majority of trials show positive trend

plants.notes: Not currently in archive — Jatamansi (related Nardostachys) is; add Valerian

fda.notes: Multiple drug interactions documented — induces CYP3A4

reviews: St John's Wort for depression (2008) — significantly more effective than placebo, comparable to standard antidepressants for mild-moderate depression; fewer side effects

plants.notes: Not in archive — add; strong evidence

fda.notes: Not approved as drug

reviews: Ginseng for cognitive function (2010) — some positive effects on cognition and behaviour
Ginseng for type 2 diabetes (2016) — modest benefit for glycemic control

plants.notes: Chinese ginseng (Panax) not in archive as separate entry; American ginseng (MMA-027) is present

fda.notes: Chamomile extract approved as natural flavoring

reviews: Chamomile for GI conditions — positive evidence for infantile colic; limited RCT evidence for other conditions

indications: symptomatic relief of GI disorders including IBS

fda.notes: Menthol approved as OTC external analgesic, oral anesthetic, and nasal decongestant; peppermint oil approved as digestive aid

reviews: Peppermint oil for IBS (2014) — significantly more effective than placebo for abdominal symptoms

who.preparation: Dried rhizome 1.5–3g/day; extract standardised to 95% curcuminoids

indications: symptomatic relief of minor digestive problems

fda.notes: Curcumin approved as food colorant (FD&C status); not approved as drug

reviews: Curcumin for osteoarthritis (2021) — significant reduction in pain and functional limitation comparable to ibuprofen
Curcumin for IBS — insufficient evidence

fda.notes: Sennosides approved as OTC stimulant laxative; in USP

reviews: Stimulant laxatives for constipation — positive evidence; sennosides specifically studied in multiple trials

plants.notes: Not in archive as separate entry — Ibn Sina documents senna; add

fda.notes: Approved OTC bulk laxative; FDA qualified health claim for soluble fibre and cardiovascular disease

reviews: Psyllium for hypercholesterolaemia — significant LDL reduction confirmed

plants.notes: Plantago ovata distinct from P. major (MMA-035) — add as separate entry

indications: lower urinary tract symptoms associated with BPH

fda.notes: Not approved as drug; extensively studied

reviews: Saw palmetto for BPH (2012, updated) — no significant difference from placebo in most outcomes in high-quality trials; earlier positive trials likely had methodological issues

plants.notes: Not in archive; North American plant with significant EMA recognition — add

indications: symptomatic relief of liver and biliary complaints

fda.notes: Not approved as drug; extensive clinical research

reviews: Milk thistle for alcoholic and/or hepatitis B or C liver disease (2005) — significant reduction in liver-related mortality; methodological concerns in some trials

plants.notes: Not in archive; North African/European plant with strong evidence base — add

fda.notes: Not approved as drug

reviews: Feverfew for migraine prevention (2004) — inconsistent results; possible modest effect for prophylaxis

plants.notes: Not in archive; Celtic/Norse plant with EMA recognition — add

indications: symptomatic treatment of chronic venous insufficiency

fda.notes: Raw seeds toxic; standardised extract studied but not FDA approved

reviews: Horse chestnut seed extract for chronic venous insufficiency (2012) — significantly improves leg pain and oedema; comparable to compression stockings in some trials

plants.notes: Not in archive; strong evidence base — add

who.preparation: Dried rhizome 250–1000mg/day; 1g fresh root in water

indications: prophylaxis of nausea and vomiting in motion sickness
symptomatic relief of nausea in pregnancy

fda.notes: Not approved as drug

reviews: Ginger for nausea and vomiting in early pregnancy (2014) — significantly reduces nausea severity; appears safe

plants.notes: Ginger not currently as a standalone archive entry — add; one of most broadly validated traditional plants

indications: adaptogenic use — traditional use not established under EU framework

fda.notes: Not approved as drug; multiple clinical trials

reviews: No Cochrane review; multiple systematic reviews positive for stress, anxiety, testosterone, and athletic performance

plants.notes: Charaka Samhita documents it; not in plant database yet — add as high priority

indications: respiratory infections — strong evidence base without formal WHO classification
elderflower for cold symptoms and fever

fda.notes: Not approved as drug

reviews: Elderberry for prevention and treatment of viral respiratory illnesses (2021) — significant reduction in duration and severity of colds and flu

indications: treatment of mild symptoms of cardiac conditions — palpitations, dyspnoea

fda.notes: Not approved as drug

reviews: Hawthorn extract for chronic heart failure (2008) — significant improvement in exercise tolerance and reduction in symptoms

plants.notes: Not in archive; Celtic/European plant with strongest cardiac evidence of any herbal medicine — add

indications: symptomatic relief of upper GI discomfort

fda.notes: Glycyrrhizin approved as flavoring; SNMC (Stronger Neominophagen C — glycyrrhizin injection) approved in Japan as anti-hepatitis drug

reviews: No single Cochrane review; multiple systematic reviews positive for peptic ulcer and gastritis

plants.notes: Licorice is the most cross-tradition plant in archive (6 traditions) but not yet a standalone plant database entry — critical to add

indications: relief of mild symptoms of mental stress and aid of sleep

fda.notes: Not approved as drug

reviews: Passiflora for anxiety disorders (2017) — comparable to low-dose oxazepam in one RCT; insufficient evidence for formal recommendation
